items.memory: When I was 0 years old, climbed the big folk's tower and stole their maps. Order broke, and something new was born.
items.reflection: The deep tunnels hold many secrets, and I will find them all.

items.memory: When I was 1 years old, swapped a horse for a goat and laughed at the trader's face. Order broke, and something new was born.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

items.memory: When I was 2 years old, sneaked into the dwarf halls for the first time and took their candles.
items.reflection: Everything is a trade if you ask the right way.

items.memory: When I was 3 years old, convinced the chieftain that I was a spirit — got extra rations for a week.
items.reflection: Never trust a goblin who says 'trust me' — that's the first rule.

items.memory: When I was 4 years old, was taught the trick of making smoke-powder by the tinker. Skigglotsnik was there too.
items.reflection: Shiny things make the world go round — and I want all of them.

items.memory: When I was 5 years old, ate a glowing mushroom and saw the colors for three days straight.
items.reflection: The best things come in small, shiny packages.

items.memory: When I was 6 years old, found a secret tunnel under the old hill and claimed it as mine. Snagmiztrak was there too.
items.reflection: Rules are for big folk — we have tricks, and tricks are better.

items.memory: When I was 7 years old, built a trap that caught a rabbit — ate it raw and felt mighty.
items.reflection: Shiny things make the world go round — and I want all of them.

items.memory: When I was 8 years old, stole a shiny thing from the big folk and hid it in my den. Snagmiztrak was there too.
items.reflection: Rules are for big folk — we have tricks, and tricks are better.

items.memory: When I was 9 years old, learned to speak in riddles and lies from the old trickster.
items.reflection: The best things come in small, shiny packages.

items.memory: Year 79 (Age of Ashen, year 18) was the season when I met my fate. The thing I cannot forget was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Turdurnaz. That year taught me that some wounds never heal. Ngrikib was there too. Glibtrakspig stood by me — a casual friend through it all.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

items.memory: Of all the years, Year 80 (Age of Ashen, year 19) was the one where I understood what power costs. The thing I cannot forget was a new site was founded: Riknobhive. The thing I cannot forget was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Turdurnaz. That year taught me that kindness matters.
items.reflection: Never trust a goblin who says 'trust me' — that's the first rule.

items.memory: I recall that in Year 81 (Age of Ashen, year 20), I made a promise. What struck me most was a new site was founded: Glotklikskab-warren. It was a year that showed me the truth. Rikzitblik was there too.
items.reflection: Everything is a trade if you ask the right way.

items.memory: The year 82 was a forgotten one. What struck me most was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Aeanoran. That year taught me that time changes everything.
items.reflection: The deep tunnels hold many secrets, and I will find them all.

items.memory: During Year 83 (Age of Ashen, year 22), I buried my dead. The most poignant moment was a new site was founded: Spigmiz-dig. The defining event was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Turdurnaz. To this day, I draw strength from it.
items.reflection: Everything is a trade if you ask the right way.

items.memory: I remember Year 84 (Age of Ashen, year 23) well — it was a season of loss. What marked that year was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. To this day, I carry its weight. The chaos of that time reshaped everything. Nackgrikzib was a casual rival — we clashed more than once.
items.reflection: Never trust a goblin who says 'trust me' — that's the first rule.

items.memory: I recall that in Year 85 (Age of Ashen, year 24), I found a hidden place. The most poignant moment was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Aeanoran. The defining event was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. I often think about the silences of that time. The chaos of that time reshaped everything. Nackgrikzib was there too.
items.reflection: Rules are for big folk — we have tricks, and tricks are better.

items.memory: Of all the years, Year 86 (Age of Ashen, year 25) was the one where I knew what hope carries. The thing I cannot forget was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. The most poignant moment was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. I often think about the sounds of that time. Order broke, and something new was born.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

items.memory: By Year 87 (Age of Ashen, year 26), the walls stood strong. The thing I cannot forget was Snagklikgob was born. The most poignant moment was Miztak was born. It was a year that left its mark. Rikzitblik was there too.
items.reflection: The deep tunnels hold many secrets, and I will find them all.

items.memory: I recall that in Year 88 (Age of Ashen, year 27), I discovered a secret. The most poignant moment was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Aeanoran. What struck me most was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. I often think about the sounds of that time. Ngrikib was there too.
items.reflection: The best things come in small, shiny packages.

items.memory: The year 89 was a hard one. What marked that year was Skabblottak was born. To this day, I wish I could forget. Order broke, and something new was born. Wigglibtock was there too. I watched Snagklikgob grow — strained, as children do.
items.reflection: Shiny things make the world go round — and I want all of them.

items.memory: At 90, a hero returned. The defining event was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. What struck me most was a great battle was fought — Miznok against Falconcon. To this day, I wonder what could have been. Ntakag was there too.
items.reflection: Rules are for big folk — we have tricks, and tricks are better.

items.memory: I recall that in Year 91 (Age of Ashen, year 30), I met a traveler from afar. What marked that year was a new site was founded: Klglibibmound. The most poignant moment was Tsnikik was born. It was a year that made me who I am.
items.reflection: The deep tunnels hold many secrets, and I will find them all.

items.memory: Year 92 (Age of Ashen, year 31) was the season when the new order began. I poisoned the water of a rival tribe and watched them wither from the hill I often think about the faces of that time. That year, the rules were unwritten.
items.reflection: The best things come in small, shiny packages.

items.memory: In Year 93 (Age of Ashen, year 32), the world felt worn and tired. The thing I cannot forget was a new site was founded: Skabsnikhollow. It was a year that left its mark. Blipzib was there too.
items.reflection: Never trust a goblin who says 'trust me' — that's the first rule.

items.memory: Year 94 (Age of Ashen, year 33) was the season when I came of age. I convinced the whole tribe to speak backwards for a full season It was a year that showed me the truth.
items.reflection: Shiny things make the world go round — and I want all of them.

items.memory: In the middle days of Year 95 (Age of Ashen, year 34), the council convened. I replaced the war banners with laundry and watched the army march wrong It was a year that broke me. Blipzib was there too.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

items.reflection: Everything is a trade if you ask the right way.

items.reflection: Rules are for big folk — we have tricks, and tricks are better.

items.memory: By Year 101 (Age of Ashen, year 40), the walls stood strong. I worked as a mushroom-farmer and made a decent living. I often think about the shadows of that time.

items.memory: By Year 102 (Age of Ashen, year 41), the nights grew colder. I painted the chieftain's face while they slept and blamed the spirits I often think about the sounds of that time.

items.memory: At 103, the temple bells fell silent. I crafted a device that could speak in voices — it told terrible lies It was a year that shaped me.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

I watched Snagklikgob grow — strained, as children do.

items.memory: During Year 141 (The Golden Times, year 18), I served at the court. I infiltrated a human fortress and stole their battle plans — sold them to three sides I often think about the silences of that time.
items.reflection: A good lie is worth more than gold, and lasts longer too.

items.memory: I spent the season guarding the walls.
A celebration was held this season — the whole town gathered.
I trained this season and learned much.
My friend Glibtrakspig shared a drink with me.
The fog this season was fierce — the elders said they had never seen it before.
The sun this season looked strange — I felt a chill.
This winter, I felt the heat settle in in Miznok.
A refugee came through Miznok this season and told of a wedding in the capital.
The old songs sing that the spring is for war.
This spring, I find myself thinking about my family far away.
The spring air makes me feel determined.
I have been praying more than usual this season — the signs are everywhere.
I wonder what the spirits of this place will bring.
This season feels precious — I fear it.
I remember this season last year — it was different then.
I need to say goodbye.
I will aid Glibtrakspig in their coming struggle.
I will attend the ritual next season.
I intend to visit the old shrine.
I will perfect my craft as a spy this coming year — I can feel myself improving.
I plan to travel to Miznok and seek my fortune there.
I fear that the old ways are fading — no one remembers them anymore.
I fear that change is coming — and I am not ready for it.
I worry about the unrest in the mountain — the deep folk are troubled.
I worry about the drought this summer — the fields are already dry.
